channel.title: CWE-527 on ZAP
channel.description: Recent content in CWE-527 on ZAP

item.title: Exposure of Git repository
item.description: Version control repositories such as CVS or git store version-specific metadata and other details within subdirectories. If these subdirectories are stored on a web server or added to an archive, then these could be used by an attacker. This information may include usernames, filenames, path root, IP addresses, and detailed ‘diff’ data about how files have been changed - which could reveal source code snippets that were never intended to be made public..

item.title: Exposure of Mercurial repository
item.description: Version control repositories such as CVS or git store version-specific metadata and other details within subdirectories. If these subdirectories are stored on a web server or added to an archive, then these could be used by an attacker. This information may include usernames, filenames, path root, IP addresses, and detailed ‘diff’ data about how files have been changed - which could reveal source code snippets that were never intended to be made public..

item.title: Exposure of SVN repository
item.description: Version control repositories such as CVS or git store version-specific metadata and other details within subdirectories. If these subdirectories are stored on a web server or added to an archive, then these could be used by an attacker. This information may include usernames, filenames, path root, IP addresses, and detailed ‘diff’ data about how files have been changed - which could reveal source code snippets that were never intended to be made public..
